data.title: Saudi Arabia Increases Crude Shipments from Red Sea Terminals
data.description: Saudi Arabia has intensified its crude oil shipments from Yanbu export terminals located on the Red Sea coast. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move to divert supplies away from the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz. This shift comes as Iran has maintained effective closure of the Strait since the end of February. The decision reflects Saudi Arabia's efforts to ensure uninterrupted oil exports amid regional tensions.

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court Overturns Copyright Verdict Against Cox Communications
data.description: The U.S. Supreme Court has overturned a copyright-infringement verdict against Cox Communications, dealing a blow to the music industry's attempts to hold internet service providers accountable for piracy. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant legal development. The decision undermines efforts by the music industry to enforce stricter liability on internet providers for illegal content shared by users. This ruling marks a pivotal moment in the ongoing debate over digital copyright enforcement and the responsibilities of internet service providers.

data.title: Dallas Fed: Survey shows WTI crude oil price expected to reach $74 per barrel by the end of 2026.
data.description: The Dallas Federal Reserve stated that a survey showed respondents expect WTI crude oil prices to reach $74 per barrel by the end of 2026, with 50% expecting no increase in the number of drilling rigs in 2026, and 47% expecting a slight or significant increase. (Jinshi)

data.title: US House Speaker: "Operation Epic Wrath" is nearing completion.
data.description: U.S. House Speaker Johnson said that our "Operation Epic Fury" is nearing completion. (Jin Shi) "Operation Epic Fury" refers to the large-scale preemptive military operation launched by the United States and Israel against Iran on February 28, 2026. The U.S. used aircraft carrier-based aircraft from the USS Ford, B-2 stealth bombers and ATACMS missiles, etc., with the aim of destroying the Iranian leadership and military targets through continuous strikes. The operation resulted in the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei. Iran then launched a counterattack codenamed "True Promise-4", which struck multiple U.S. bases in the Middle East. [3] [5] The conflict caused casualties among U.S. personnel and triggered disruptions to the global energy supply chain and a rise in oil prices.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC Amid Market Activity
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Whale Alert has reported that the USDC Treasury has minted 250 million USDC. This development comes as part of ongoing market activities involving stablecoins. The minting of USDC, a widely used stablecoin pegged to the U.S. dollar, is often indicative of increased demand or strategic financial maneuvers within the cryptocurrency market. Such actions can influence liquidity and trading dynamics, impacting investor strategies and market sentiment. The minting process is closely monitored by market participants, as it can signal shifts in capital flows and potential changes in market conditions.

data.title: Data: Bitcoin call options traded nearly $850 million today, accounting for over 35% of total trading volume.
data.description: Greeks.live posted on the X platform that nearly $850 million worth of Bitcoin call options were traded today, accounting for over 35% of the total, mainly due to rollover. Influenced by the Middle East situation and US stock market trends, this final week has seen significant volatility, but last-minute sell-offs to capitalize on the major settlement day remain a high-probability trading strategy. It is understood that this Friday is the quarterly settlement day, with over 40% of options expiring, and the $75,000 level remains a strong resistance point.

data.title: Potential Strike Looms for New York City Apartment Building Staff
data.description: Residents of New York City apartment buildings may face a strike by building staff as early as next month. Bloomberg posted on X that the union representing these workers is currently in negotiations with an advisory board representing building owners to establish a new labor contract. The discussions aim to address various employment terms and conditions, but if an agreement is not reached, a strike could disrupt services for residents across the city. The potential strike highlights ongoing labor tensions in the real estate sector, affecting both workers and residents.

data.title: Franklin Templeton Collaborates with Ondo Finance for 24/7 Tokenized ETF Trading
data.description: Franklin Templeton has announced a collaboration with Ondo Finance to introduce tokenized versions of its exchange-traded funds (ETFs). Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this initiative allows these ETFs to be traded continuously through cryptocurrency wallets, eliminating the need for traditional brokerage accounts and the constraints of limited trading hours that have long characterized fund investing. This move signifies a shift towards more flexible and accessible investment options in the financial markets.

data.title: The Marshall Islands' universal basic income project receives $3 million in investment from individuals with a crypto background.
data.description: M1X Global, the company behind the Marshall Islands' Universal Basic Income (UBI) project, announced the completion of a $3 million angel round of financing. Investors include several industry executives with crypto and finance backgrounds. The new funds will be used to promote the development and application of the USDM1 digital sovereign bond. USDM1 is a sovereign financial instrument pegged to the US dollar, designed to support local residents' participation in the UBI program. This asset was listed on Stellar in December 2025 and is reportedly planned to expand into the institutional market and be used as high-quality collateral in the future. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Meta Restructures, Cuts Hundreds of Jobs Across Multiple Divisions
data.description: Meta is undergoing a significant restructuring, resulting in the elimination of several hundred positions across various departments. Bloomberg posted on X that the affected teams include sales, recruiting, and the Reality Labs hardware division. This move is part of Meta's broader strategy to streamline operations and enhance efficiency. The restructuring reflects the company's ongoing efforts to adapt to changing market conditions and optimize its workforce. Meta's decision to reduce its workforce comes amid a challenging economic environment, prompting many tech companies to reassess their organizational structures and resource allocations.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC on Solana
data.description: On March 25, USDC Treasury minted 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain. According to PANews, this transaction occurred at 22:03 (UTC+8) and was valued at approximately $249.997125 million at the time. Whale Alert reported the minting, highlighting the significant movement of digital currency within the Solana network.

data.title: Rising Prices in Mexico City Lead to Building Evictions
data.description: Evictions of entire buildings in Mexico City have sparked outrage as rising prices force residents out. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing discontent among locals who are being displaced due to increasing costs. The situation has drawn attention to the broader issue of affordability in the city, where many are struggling to keep up with the escalating expenses. The evictions underscore the challenges faced by residents in maintaining their homes amid a rapidly changing economic landscape.

data.title: Empery Digital Raises $25 Million Through Share Sale and Bitcoin Transaction
data.description:
Empery Digital has successfully raised $25 million by selling 4,638,219 common shares at a price of $5.39 each. According to NS3.AI, the company also issued 4,638,219 warrants with a four-year term. The funds raised are intended to repay repurchase loans.

In a separate transaction last week, Empery Digital sold 63 Bitcoin at an average price of $72,791, leaving approximately 3,439 BTC on its balance sheet.

data.title: Energy Infrastructure Faces Damage Amid Ongoing Iran Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran, now in its fourth week, has resulted in significant damage to numerous energy infrastructures. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that missile and drone strikes have impacted dozens of refineries, oil fields, gas plants, and ports. The continuous attacks have raised concerns over the stability and security of energy supplies in the region. As the situation develops, the global energy market remains on alert, monitoring the potential implications for supply chains and pricing.

data.title: Fitch Predicts Ongoing Policy Uncertainty to Impact U.S. Commodity Trade Until 2026
data.description: Fitch Ratings has forecasted that policy uncertainty will continue to affect U.S. commodity trade through 2026. According to Jin10, the agency highlights concerns over regulatory changes and geopolitical tensions as key factors contributing to this prolonged uncertainty. The analysis suggests that these elements may hinder trade growth and impact market stability. Fitch's outlook underscores the need for clarity in policy direction to mitigate potential disruptions in the commodity sector. The agency advises stakeholders to remain vigilant and adaptable to evolving conditions in the global trade environment.

data.title: Italy and Spain Compete for North African Gas Amid Middle Eastern Supply Disruptions
data.description:
Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is working to increase gas imports from Algeria as Italy and Spain compete to secure more energy resources from North Africa. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move as both countries aim to mitigate the impact of reduced supplies from the Middle East due to the ongoing conflict involving Iran.

The disruption in Middle Eastern energy supplies has prompted European nations to seek alternative sources to ensure energy security. Algeria, with its significant gas reserves, has become a focal point for Italy and Spain as they look to diversify their energy imports.

Meloni's efforts are part of a broader strategy to strengthen Italy's energy ties with North Africa, a region that has become increasingly important in the wake of geopolitical tensions affecting traditional supply routes. Spain, similarly, is looking to bolster its energy partnerships in the region to safeguard its energy needs.

The competition between Italy and Spain underscores the growing importance of North African energy resources in the European energy landscape, as countries seek to reduce their reliance on volatile Middle Eastern supplies.

data.title: SIX CEO Remains Optimistic About IPO Prospects Despite Iran Conflict
data.description: The CEO of SIX, which operates the Swiss and Spanish stock exchanges, remains hopeful that this year will see an improvement in initial public offerings compared to last year. Bloomberg posted on X, however, that the ongoing conflict in Iran is prompting companies to reconsider their IPO plans. Despite the geopolitical tensions, the exchange operator's leader maintains a positive outlook for the market's potential recovery.

data.title: Coinbase and Chainlink expand DeFi application scenarios by putting order books and derivatives data on-chain.
data.description: Coinbase announced that, for the first time, it has directly pushed its exchange market data onto the blockchain via Chainlink DataLink, including order books and derivatives data such as futures and perpetual contracts. This integration covers data streams from Coinbase International Exchange and Coinbase Derivatives Exchange, enabling on-chain protocols and applications to access and verify various market information, including perpetual contracts, stocks, and commodities, in real time. Coinbase stated that this move will not only help it commercialize its core market data resources and advance its "all-in-one exchange" and comprehensive prime brokerage strategy, but will also provide institutional-grade benchmark data for DeFi and traditional financial developers, supporting application innovations such as derivatives and tokenized assets. Chainlink pointed out that this collaboration is expected to provide a more secure and reliable data infrastructure for on-chain finance, driving the development of the next-generation financial system. (The Block)

data.title: New York Lawmakers Propose Tax Increase on Startup Investment Gains
data.description: New York lawmakers have introduced a proposal to increase taxes on investment gains from startups, sparking opposition from the tech industry. Bloomberg posted on X that the industry argues the tax hike could deter venture capital investments in the state, potentially impacting the growth of new businesses. The proposal has ignited a debate over its potential effects on New York's economic landscape, with tech leaders expressing concerns about the long-term implications for innovation and entrepreneurship.

data.title: Iran Calls for Legally Binding Mechanism to Prevent Future Wars, Iranian Media Reports
data.description: Iran has called for the establishment of a legally binding mechanism to ensure that wars do not recur, according to Iranian media reports. This move underscores Iran's commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region. The proposal highlights the importance of international cooperation in preventing conflicts and fostering a secure global environment.
